# Hojun Song / 송호준
> Artist and engineer working at the intersection of art, technology, and social commentary.
> 예술과 기술, 사회적 코멘터리의 교차점에서 작업하는 아티스트이자 엔지니어.

Website: https://hojunsong.com
Machine-readable data: https://hojunsong.com/data/works.json
License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)

---

## Biography (English)

b. 1978, South Korea

Hojun Song is an artist and engineer whose practice sits at the intersection of art, technology, and social commentary. Working across DIY engineering, performance, mass production, live streaming, and music, he creates absurd yet functional objects and systems that question how society constructs heroes, produces knowledge, and surrenders diversity to efficiency.

In 2013, Song launched OSSI-1 (Open Source Satellite Initiative) from Baikonur, Kazakhstan, the first satellite ever built and launched by an individual. Rather than celebrating the achievement, he turned the project into a critique of heroism and nationalism in science, funding it through T-shirt sales and documenting the process through performance and music. The satellite was less the point than the questions it raised: who gets to access space, and why do we need heroes to do it?

This skepticism runs through his entire body of work. The Strongest Weapon in the World (2007–2010) is an indestructible object that does not attack; when struck with a hammer, it says "I love you." Uranium Necklace (2010–2020) offers radioactive jewelry as a way to "taste death." LED That Blinks Once Every 100 Years (2010–2026), rejected by a committee that wanted it to blink annually, finally emitted its first light on January 14, 2026. The next blink is scheduled for 2126.

His ongoing series Don't Compress Me (2009–) is a global competition where participants move for 10 seconds to resist video compression algorithms. Random movements should defeat codecs like H.264, but humans consciously trying to be random cannot truly be random. The competition, held in Basel, Goa, Rome, Seoul, and Daejeon, is framed as a search for "a human who can live beyond the predictions of artificial intelligence." ANTI-A.I. Vocalization Techniques (2018) explores how we might fool speech recognition while remaining understandable to other humans.

Song's work extends into experimental music with dssb, a death metal duo formed in response to the 2014 Sewol ferry disaster, and gold & silver, an improvisation duo that charges negative admission so they can fail freely. Future School Food Foraging – Seaweed (2021), presented at the 17th Venice Architecture Biennale, connects Korean seaweed culture to climate change and diaspora. Time to Leave the Land (2020–2022) uses a sailboat as a floating studio for science fiction writing.

His work has been presented at the Venice Architecture Biennale, Poznan Mediations Biennale, Zero1 Biennial, Leeum Samsung Museum of Art, MMCA Seoul, and Art Sonje Center. He has spoken at MIT, RCA London, and Wired UK Conference, and has been featured in BBC, Wired, New Scientist, and Reuters.

---

## 약력 (한국어)

1978년생, 한국

송호준은 예술과 기술, 사회적 코멘터리의 교차점에서 작업하는 아티스트이자 엔지니어다. DIY 공학, 퍼포먼스, 대량생산, 라이브 스트리밍, 음악을 넘나들며 부조리하지만 작동하는 사물과 시스템을 만든다. 그의 작업은 사회가 영웅을 만들어내는 방식, 지식을 생산하는 방식, 다양성을 효율에 내어주는 방식에 질문을 던진다.

2013년 카자흐스탄 바이코누르에서 OSSI-1 (오픈소스 인공위성 프로젝트) 을 발사했다. 개인이 인공위성을 만들어 발사한 세계 최초의 사례였다. 그는 이 성취를 축하하는 대신, 과학에서의 영웅주의와 국가주의를 비판하는 프로젝트로 전환했다. 티셔츠 판매로 자금을 조달하고, 퍼포먼스와 음악으로 과정을 기록했다. 위성을 만든 건 그 자체가 목적이라기보다, 우주에 대한 접근이 왜 특정한 사람들에게만 허용되는지 그리고 왜 우주 과학은 영웅 서사가 필요한지 묻고 싶었기 때문이다. 

비슷한 관심이 다른 작업에도 이어진다. 세상에서 가장 강한 무기(2007–2010)는 파괴되지 않지만 공격하지 않는 물체다. 망치로 내리치면 "사랑해"라고 말한다. 우라늄 목걸이(2010–2020)는 방사성 보석으로 "죽음을 맛보는" 경험을 제안한다. 100년에 한 번 깜빡이는 LED(2010–2026)는 1년에 한 번으로 바꾸라는 심사위원단의 요구를 거부한 끝에, 2026년 1월 14일 첫 빛을 냈다. 다음 깜빡임은 2126년이다.

진행 중인 시리즈 압축하지마(2009–)는 참가자들이 10초간 움직여 비디오 압축 알고리즘에 저항하는 글로벌 대회다. 무작위 움직임은 H.264 같은 코덱을 무력화할 수 있지만, 의식적으로 무작위적이려는 인간은 진정으로 무작위적일 수 없다. 바젤, 고아, 로마, 서울, 대전에서 열린 이 대회는 "인공지능의 예측에서 벗어나 살 수 있는 인간"을 찾기위해 만들어졌다. 반인공지능 발성법(2018)은 음성인식을 속이면서도 다른 인간에게는 이해 가능한 말하기 방식을 탐구하는 대회이다.

음악 활동으로는 2014년 세월호 참사에 대한 응답으로 결성한 데스메탈 듀오 dssb, 관객에게 돈을 지불하는 마이너스 입장료로 자유롭게 실패하는 즉흥 듀오 gold & silver가 있다. 제17회 베니스 건축 비엔날레에서 발표한 미래학교 식량 채집 – 해초(2021)는 한국의 해초 문화를 기후변화, 디아스포라와 연결한다. 이제는 육지를 떠날 때(2020–2022)는 요트를 떠다니는 스튜디오로 삼아 SF 글쓰기를 시도한 프로젝트다.

베니스 건축 비엔날레, 포츠난 미디어 비엔날레, Zero1 비엔니얼, 리움삼성미술관, 국립현대미술관, 아트선재센터 등에서 작품을 발표했다. MIT, RCA 런던, Wired UK 컨퍼런스에서 강연했으며, BBC, Wired, New Scientist, Reuters 등에 소개되었다.

### 2026
- 100년에 한번 깜박이는 LED, 갤럭시 코퍼레이션, 서울
### 2025

- 인류를 위하여 방구 끼지마, 부산현대미술관, 부산
- 압축하지마 세계대회 로마, Videocittà, 로마
- Cabinet of the Ephemeral, 아트센터나비, 서울

- 베니스 건축 비엔날레 한국관 오프닝, 서울 & 베니스
- 방구를 참아라, 코믹 환경 웹 모바일 게임, 디렉터
- 이제는 육지를 떠날 때 - 친환경 바다 예술 코인 발행, 서해
- 미래학교 식량채집 - 해조류 편, 베니스 건축 비엔날레, 완도
- 압축하지마 온라인, 메이킹레모네이드 전, 서울
- 압축하지마: 추상, 신세계 갤러리, 부산
- 이제는 육지를 떠날 때 - 더 비기닝, 디스위켄드룸, 서울
### 2020	
- 위대한폭발: 코로나19, 김희수 아트센터, 서울
- 압축하지마, 오픈미디어아트페스티발, 서울
- 메타퍼포먼스: 미래극장, 경기도

---

## Works / 작품

### Open Source Satellite Initiative / 오픈소스 인공위성 프로젝트 (OSSI)
**Year / 연도:** 2008–2018
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/open-source-satellite-initiative/

The OSSI-1 satellite was launched by Hojun Song in April 2013 at Baikonur, Kazakhstan—the first satellite ever built and launched by an individual.
송호준 작가는 2013년 카자흐스탄 바이코누르에서 OSSI-1 인공위성을 발사하였다. 이 인공위성은 역사상 개인이 만들어 쏘아올린 세계 최초의 인공위성으로 주목받았으나, 작가는 이 상황을 과학에서의 영웅주의와 국가주의, 오늘날 아마추어의 역할, 민감한 기술의 공유 등을 풍자적으로 이야기하는 기회로 삼으려 하였다.

Hojun Song launched the OSSI-1 satellite in April 2013 at Baikonur, Kazakhstan—the first satellite ever built and launched by an individual.

While audiences are drawn to this achievement, Hojun Song shifts attention to broader themes: heroism and nationalism in science, the role of amateurs today, and the sharing of sensitive technologies. He explores these ideas through comedic and satirical performances.

Korean Artist + UK Magazine -> U.S. Flag behind.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- Labor Of Love, Revisited, Arko Art Center, Seoul, 2011
- Zero1 Biennial, San Jose, California, 2012
  Presenting Open Source Satellite Initiative project at the Zero1 Biennial in San Jose, California. NASA Ames Center was near by so many scientis stopped by to talk.
- Art Spectrum, Leeum Museum of Art, Seoul, 2014
  The core idea of the OSSI satellite project is to share the entire process of building and launching a satellite. Therefore, even though it was presented in a renowned exhibition such as Art Spectrum, the work was presented in a deliberately approachable and accessible manner. The main idea behind the presentation format was inspired by kiosks found in local community offices and post offices. 
  By encouraging active audience participation—watching videos and solving quizzes—the project aimed to help viewers realize that the technologies discussed in the satellite project are in fact connected to broader themes such as society, politics, and people’s dreams.
  Before the exhibition, more than 100 elementary school students in Seoul sent handwritten letters titled “To Scientist Hojun Song, Who Launched a Satellite into Space.” The students and their science teacher were invited to the art exhibition, where a gathering was held to share conversations together.
- Festival Bom, Seoul, 2014
  Open Source Satellite Initiative project presented at Festival BO:M 2014 as rap music performance.
- Objectology 2, Gwacheon, MMCA, 2015
  OSSI-1 Satellite Manufacturing Techniques, 2015
  This work presents stories about manufacturing techniques based on practical knowledge of satellite construction. Rather than focusing on the operating principles of satellites, it highlights hands-on processes such as wire selection, wire stripping, soldering, aluminum machining, spring fabrication, antenna making, and selecting adhesives capable of functioning in space. 
  These forms of practical knowledge—often referred to as know-how—are much harder to find than theoretical principles organized in textbooks. The exhibition presents actual fabricated satellite components alongside stories of the people, communities, and online knowledge encountered throughout the production process.
  Audiences can see the explanations of the items via website.
- OSSI Goods, Various, 2008–2018
- OSSI Videos, Various, 2008–2018

---

### On Off Everything / On Off Everything
**Year / 연도:** 2018–2019
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/on-off-everything/

Audiences bring their own electronic devices to this project. Each device has its own timing, lights, and sounds when switching on and off.
On Off Everything은 관객들이 가져온 전자장치들을 반복적으로 켜고 끄는 프로젝트이다. 전자장치는 다양한 부품들로 이뤄진 하나의 시스템이며 각각의 장치들마다 켜고 꺼지는 타이밍, 불빛과 소리들이 모두 다르다. 모든 장치들이 메인 전원에 연결되고 그 메인 전원을 주기적으로 켜고 끄기를 반복하면 연결된 장치들의 오디오 비주얼 조합은 예측 불가능한 새로운 형태가 된다.

Audiences bring their own electronic devices to this project. Each device is a system with its own timing, lights, and sounds when switching on and off.

When all devices are connected to the installation and the central power cycles on and off, unexpected audiovisual combinations emerge.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- Artience Daejeon, Daejeon, 2019
- On Off Everything, Mumokjeok, Seoul, 2019
  On Off Everything, a new project by media artist Hojun Song, is an exhibition that repeatedly turns electronic and mechanical devices on and off. The artist’s core approach—creating narratives through technology—lies in hoping that the simultaneous switching on and off of multiple devices resonates with the many stories of the world. In this exhibition, Song explores unexpected synesthetic effects through combinations and operations of various devices.
  The first edition of On Off Everything begins at the space “Mumokjeok.”
  Fifty power outlets are waiting for you.
  - Dates: July 26–28
  - Opening Party: July 26, 8:00 PM (DJ Jeon yonghyeon)

---

### Time To Leave The Land / 이제는 육지를 떠날 때
**Year / 연도:** 2020–2022
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/time-to-leave-the-land/

This project uses a sailboat as a floating, isolated studio for near-future storytelling. It explores off-grid ocean living in an era of pandemics and virtual reality.
'이제는 육지를 떠날 때' 프로젝트는 부유하며 고립된 상황에서 근미래 스토리텔링 작업을 할 수 있는 스튜디오를 만들기 위해 세일 요트를 이용한다. 가상현실과 전염병 시대의 바다에서의 오프그리드한 삶을 주된 주제로 삼고, 과학자, 예술가, 음악가, 저술가 등과 먼 바다를 항해하며 공상과학 진(Zine)을 집필한다.

This project uses a sailboat as a floating, isolated studio for near-future storytelling. It explores off-grid ocean living in an era of pandemics and virtual reality.

Scientists, artists, musicians, and writers sail together to create a short science fiction zine. Conversations on board are live-streamed for real-time collaboration with audiences. The sailing and publishing happen quarterly.

---

### Future School Food Foraging - Seaweed / 미래학교 식량채집 - 해조류 편
**Year / 연도:** 2021
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/future-school-food-foraging-seaweed/

Seaweeds like Miyeok and Dasima are now considered among the most invasive species globally, yet they are traditional postpartum food in Korea.
미역과 다시마와 같은 해조류의 포자들은 바다의 흐름에 맡겨져 떠내려 가다 정착할 뿐이다. 세계적으로 가장 침략적인 외래종으로 분류되어 있으나 한국에선 생명의 탄생과 함께하는 음식이기도 하다. 보잘것없다고 생각되는 해조류를 통해 미래식량, 기후위기, 탈식민, 그리고 디아스포라와 같은 국제사회의 여러 이슈를 다룰 수 있다.

Seaweeds like Miyeok (Undaria pinnatifida) and Dasima (Laminaria japonica) travel with ocean currents and settle wherever they land. Now considered among the most invasive species globally, they are also traditional postpartum food in Korea.

We know that ocean seaweed captures more carbon than the Amazon rainforest, yet few international institutions officially recognize this.

It's striking how this seemingly insignificant seaweed connects to major global issues: future food, climate change, decolonization, and diaspora.

## Credits
17th Venice Architecture Biennale Korean Pavilion Program
Yaksan-myun, Wando-gun, South Korea

- Project director - Hojun Song
- Artist - Hojun Song, Leo James Smith (Italy)
- Chef - Hyunchung Kim (Tongyeong Owall)
- Wando Yaksan coordinator - Sungjin Kim
- Photography - Hojun Song, Wontae Suh, Dongkoo Jung
- Livestreaming - Hojun Song
- Accomodation & goat - Dowon Pension

Foraging guide 
- Wando county office 
- Wando seaweed Expo team 
- Tongyeong lilibongbong Cafe - Jiyoung Kim
- Science Bookshop GALDAR Manager - Miyoung Lee
- Ahn Eun Me Future School Movement - Eunme Ahn 
- Ahn Eun Me Future School Movement - Marie Jean
- Ahn Eun Me Future School Movement-  Hyegyung
- Climate Citizen 3.5 Direcotr - Hyewon Lee
- Art X Tech Seoul
- Venice Biennale Food Team Director - Hyewon Lee
- Venice Biennale Korean Pavilion Office - Jiyoon Ahn
- Venice Biennale Korean Pavilion Director - Hyewon Shin

---

### Don't Compress Me / 압축하지마
**Year / 연도:** 2009–
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/dont-compress-me/

Participants try to create movements for 10 seconds that resist video compression.
'압축하지마'는 동영상 압축 알고리즘에 나의 존재가 쉽게 압축당하지 않도록 10초 동안 최선을 다해 움직임을 만들어 내는 작업이다. 가장 압축이 덜 된 사람이 우승하는 방식이다. 무작위로 움직여 H.264와 같은 압축 코덱의 효율성을 방해해야 하지만 이를 의식하는 순간 랜덤이 아니게 되는 아이러니에 빠져든다. 참가자들은 작업을 통해 인공지능의 예측 범위에서 벗어날 수 있는 몸짓과 생각을 고민한다.

> “How can we think and act In the age of A.I. ?”

Participants try to create movements for 10 seconds that resist video compression. They stand before a camera and record; the person whose video stays largest after compression wins.

Random movements should defeat codecs like H.264, but there's an irony: once people consciously try to be random, they can't truly be random.

The competition has been held all over the world, including:
* Basel, Switzerland 🇨🇭
* Goa, India 🇮🇳
* Rome, Italy 🇮🇹
* Seoul and Daejeon, South Korea 🇰🇷

In 2022, Don't Compress Me World Competition started.
 
The artist Hojun Song says he started the competition to find a human who can live beyond the predictions of artificial intelligence.

The competition will continue to be held in various cities around the world. 

The winner in each city will receive 1 Ethereum as a prize, and the winner of the World Series to be held in Korea will receive 1 Bitcoin.

> Join the “Don’t Compress Me” competition to become a New Human. 

The work, contemplated since 2009, was first realized and presented at Future Now, Berlin, 2015.

Unlike the regular "Don't Compress Me" competition where participants use their entire body, this version requires participants to move only their head for 10 seconds to resist video compression.

Artists from various countries participated in the competition, and certain patterns seemed to emerge in their movements and sound.

At "Don't Compress," shown at Unfold X in late 2025, many people participated in groups. Individuals could not overcome the dynamics of the group.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- Blackout Basel, Basel, Switzerland, 2015
  Don't Compress Me Competition Head Only Version at Blackout Basel, Basel, Switzerland, 2015
  Unlike the regular "Don't Compress Me" competition where participants use their entire body, this version requires participants to move only their head for 10 seconds to resist video compression.
  Artists from various countries participated in the competition, and certain patterns seemed to emerge in their movements and sound.
- Future Now, SomoS Art House, Berlin, 2015
- The Story of Space, Goa, India, 2018
- Echo Chamber, Sound Effects Seoul, Seoul, 2019
- Open Media Art Festival, Seoul, 2020
- Don't Compress Me Abstracted, Shinsegae Gallery, Busan, 2021
  The more one tries to reveal existence, the more it appears in high resolution. 
  If you remain still, you are nothing more than a pixel at the lowest resolution.
- Making Lemonade, Making Lemonade, Seoul, 2021
- Don't Compress Me World Competition Seoul, Mother Offline, 2022
  Artist Hojun Song's
  🌐 Don’t Compress Me World Competition Begins 📢
  👽 Finding a new type of human in the era of artificial intelligence and uncertainty.
  🏆 Awarding 1 BTC to the final winner of the least compressed at  the world competition after moving the body for 10 seconds in front of the camera.
  You need to move as fast and randomly as possible with colorful outfits in order not to get compressed.
  - The 1st City:  Seoul (Prize 1 ETH)
  - When: 7.22 Fri 6PM Registration, 8PM Competition
  - Venue: Mother Offline Yongsan-gu, Hannam-dong, Itaewon-ro 55na-gil, 6
  - Music: DJ Maktoop, DJ DGURU 
  - Sponsor: NFT Mania, Mother Media
- DCMWC Rome, Videocittà, Rome, 2023
- Pack-Unpack, Total Museum, Seoul, 2024
- Unfold X, Culture Station Seoul 284, 2025
  Interactive installation presented at the Unfold X 2025. Participants stood in front of a camera and moved freely for 10 seconds. 
  More than 1000 people participated this time. Many group sessions were recorded. Individuals could not overcome the dynamics of the group.

---

### The Great Explosion / 위대한 폭발
**Year / 연도:** 2020
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/the-great-explosion/

This project collects participants' voices as material for an explosion simulation.
위대한 폭발 프로젝트는 사람들의 목소리를 재료로 사용한 폭발 시뮬레이션이다. 폭발에서 날아가는 수많은 입자들의 에너지와 경로를 사람들의 목소리에 매핑하여 응축시키고 폭발시킨다. 시뮬레이션된 재난과 코로나19와 같은 실재하는 재난 상황을 생각해본다.

This project collects participants' voices as material for an explosion simulation. Voices are mapped to fragment energies and trajectories, then detonated.

The noise of all voices playing at once, the overloaded computer, and visuals shifting between order and chaos invite us to think about the space between simulated disasters and real ones like COVID-19.

---

### ANTI-A.I. Vocalization Techniques / 반인공지능 발성법
**Year / 연도:** 2018
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/anti-ai-vocalization-techniques/

How can we fool A.I. speech recognition while remaining understandable to humans—without changing what we say?
사람은 알아들을 수 있는데 인공지능 기반의 음성인식 시스템을 속일 수 있는 발성법에 대하여 연구하는 작업이다. 억양을 바꾸거나 음절 사이사이에 화이트 노이즈와 같은 소리를 집어넣어 발성한 후 구글의 음성인식 시스템을 속일 수 있었다. 인공지능은 다양성을 파괴한다. 인공지능이 만드는 편견을 거부하자.

How can we fool A.I. speech recognition while remaining understandable to humans—without changing what we say?

By altering intonation and inserting white noise between syllables, we can deceive Google Cloud Speech Recognition.

A.I. kills diversity. Deny the bias.

---

### gold & silver / gold & silver (즉흥 듀오)
**Year / 연도:** 2017–
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/gold-and-silver/

gold & silver is a two-member experimental improvisation band: Hojun Song and Sangback Choe.
gold & silver는 송호준과 최상백으로 구성된 즉흥 실험 음악 밴드이다. 무엇을 왜 어떻게 실험할지 관객들과 적극적으로 소통하는 친절한 실험 음악 공연 밴드이다. 실패에 대해 죄책감을 느끼지 않고 실험하기 위해 관객들에게 돈을 주는 공연을 해오고 있다.

gold & silver is a two-member experimental improvisation band: Hojun Song and Sangback Choe. The band makes experimental music accessible by actively explaining what they're doing and why during performances.

They share improvised song titles and the thinking behind them, helping audiences imagine along. When it works, audiences can even sing along to improvised sounds.

The band has charged negative admission—paying audiences to attend—so they can fail freely without guilt. Current setup: modular synthesizers and acoustic drums.

[Apple Music](https://music.apple.com/kr/album/hello-world-ep/1802809497?l=en-GB)
[Spotify Music](https://open.spotify.com/album/4frAnlsHYnjVGZuOjjS2BU)

---

### GODLED Electronics / GODLED 일렉트로닉스
**Year / 연도:** 2010–2016
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/godled-electronics/

GODLED Electronics is Hojun Song's company for mass-producing God-shaped LED electronics.
GODLED 일렉트로닉스는 송호준 작가가 세운 신(神) 모양의 LED를 대량생산하는 회사이다. 현재 제품은 부처(LG-BUD-6036), 예수(LW-JES-7620), 마리아(LB-MAR-4125), 그리고 가네시(LR-GAN-6036)이다.

GODLED Electronics is Hojun Song's company for mass-producing God-shaped LED electronics. The idea began in 2010; the first prototypes appeared in 2016. 

Current lineup: Buddha (LG-BUD-6036), Jesus (LW-JES-7620), Mary (LB-MAR-4125), and Ganesh (LR-GAN-6036), with production planned for Q3 2021.

For this brief time, you cannot see the lights but can count photons with photomultiplier tube.

---

### For Humanity - Einstein Merong / 인류를 위하여 - 아인슈타인 메롱
**Year / 연도:** 2016
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/for-humanity-einstein-merong/

Even after the Trinity test revealed the bomb's power, and even after Germany's defeat, the Manhattan Project continued. The question remains: couldn't these geniuses have stopped it?
트리니티 실험을 통해 핵폭탄의 파괴력을 확인했고 독일이 항복했음에도 불구하고 맨해튼 프로젝트는 멈추지 않았고 일본에 2개의 핵폭탄을 투하하였다. 정말 그 수많은 천재들 중 한 명이라도 폭탄을 투하하기 전 프로젝트를 중단할 수 없었을까?

Even after the Trinity test revealed the bomb's power, and even after Germany's defeat, the Manhattan Project continued—dropping nuclear bombs on two Japanese cities. Many scientists, including Einstein, later expressed regret. But the question remains: couldn't these geniuses have stopped it before the bombing? 

In this installation, as viewers examine a tiny, gold-framed text about the Manhattan Project up close, a nearby video of Einstein transitions from his normal expression to his famous tongue-out gesture—supposedly made "for humanity." Gotcha!

---

### Divided We Stand, United We Fall / 흩어지면 살고 뭉치면 죽는다
**Year / 연도:** 2015
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/divided-we-stand-united-we-fall/

Divided We Stand, United We Fall. The deconstruction of a hero. A manifesto for diversity. ANTI-A.I.
흩어지면 살고 뭉치면 죽는다. 영웅의 해체. 다양성 선언. 반 인공지능.

Divided We Stand, United We Fall. The deconstruction of a hero. A manifesto for diversity. ANTI-A.I.

---

### Poomba / 품바
**Year / 연도:** 2015
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/poomba/

Winner of the Ilmac Art Prize 2015, Hojun Song's solo exhibition featured no physical artworks. The poomba was a Korean figure: satirist, clown, beggar, and street monk.
2015년 일맥 아트 프라이즈 수상자로 선정된 송호준 작가의 개인전. 하지만 어떠한 물리적인 작업도 선보이지 않았다. '품바'는 한국 고려시대 말에 등장한 거지이기도 하지만 광대이며 존경받는 거리의 승려인 한 풍자 시인이다. 스스로를 낮추면서도 영웅들을 해체하는 영웅이 품바였다.

Winner of the Ilmac Art Prize 2015, Hojun Song's solo exhibition featured no physical artworks. Deconstructing heroes and restoring romance collided—the artist could only ask questions, since making and showing things felt like violence. The poomba was a Korean figure from the late Goryeo dynasty: satirist, clown, beggar, and street monk, respected for wisdom. A self-deprecating, hero-deconstructing hero. The exhibition explored what a future poomba might be. Four talks and four workshops were held at the gallery and a nearby construction site, all live-streamed. Activities included camping and swimming at the construction site, with nightly gatherings for drinks.

---

### LED That Blinks Once Every 100 Years / 100년에 한번 깜박이는 LED
**Year / 연도:** 2010–2015, 2025
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/led-that-blinks-once-every-100-years/

A small, cheap 5mm LED that blinks once every 100 years. The project was rejected after Hojun Song refused to make the LED blink every year instead.
작고 값싼 5mm 직경의 LED가 100년에 한 번 깜박인다. 100년간 동작할 전원으로 핵전지가 사용되고 열 시뮬레이션을 통해 패시브 냉각 시스템이 디자인되었다. 100년에 한 번 깜박이는 LED는 1년마다 한 번씩 켜달라는 공공조형물 심사위원단의 제안을 거절하여 최종 탈락하였다.

A small, cheap 5mm LED that blinks once every 100 years. The idea began in 2010 and nearly became a public art project in 2015. A two-meter spherical structure would house an LED module in an inert gas-filled vacuum chamber. Nuclear batteries would provide 100 years of power, with a passive heating system designed through thermal simulation. The project was rejected after Hojun Song refused the committee's proposal to make it blink every year.

Twelve years later in 2026, [the first version](/works/led-that-blinks-once-every-100-years/2025-Ver001/) was built. 

> The first light was emitted at 8:00 PM on January 14, 2026. The next blink will occur at the same time on **January 14, 2126**.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- Public Installation, Naju, 2014
- Galaxy Corporation Collection, Seoul, 2025
  > The LED blinks only once every 100 years. The first light was emitted at 8:00 PM on January 14, 2026. The next blink will occur at the same time on **January 14, 2126**.
   The average data retention period of semiconductor electronics is merely 20 years. All our digital records could be lost in an instant.
  The most powerful thing in the world is a record that endures the longest. [The OSSI satellite](/works/open-source-satellite-initiative/) project and [The Strongest Weapon in the World](/works/the-strongest-weapon-in-the-world/) emerged from this belief.

---

### dssb / dssb (데스메탈 듀오)
**Year / 연도:** 2014
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/dssb/

dssb is a death metal-inspired duo: Hojun Song and Jinhwa Jeong. The band formed in response to the Sewol ferry disaster in 2014.
dssb는 송호준, 정진화로 구성된 데스메탈 같은 2인 밴드이다. 밴드는 2014년 세월호 사고와 무기력했던 한국 사회에 영향을 받아 시작되었다.

dssb is a death metal-inspired duo: Hojun Song and Jinhwa Jeong. The band formed in response to the Sewol ferry disaster, which killed 304 passengers and left Korean society feeling helpless in 2014.

[Apple Music](https://music.apple.com/kr/album/beautiful-nation-single/1561357862?l=en-GB)

---

### Hello 90 / 안녕하십니까 90
**Year / 연도:** 2014
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/hello-90/

In Korea, bowing expresses greeting or gratitude. But when combined with extreme machismo, it becomes a fear response. Hello 90 is an interactive video installation.
인사를 하거나 고마움을 표시할 때 한국에서는 고개를 숙인다. 하지만 이 고개 숙임은 한국의 극단적인 마초 문화와 연결되면 강자에 대한 두려움의 반응으로 해석할 수 있다. '안녕하십니까 90'은 공간에 설치된 센서가 인식되면 마주하고 있는 영상에서 큰 소리와 90도 안녕하십니까를 보여주는 인터랙티브 비디오 설치작업이다.

In Korea, bowing expresses greeting or gratitude. But when combined with extreme machismo, it becomes a fear response. A normal bow is 30-40 degrees; the extreme macho bow is a full 90 degrees—faster, with a louder greeting. This bowing style is common among gangsters and military personnel, but also in college clubs and among K-pop idols. 

Hello 90 is an interactive video installation where people on screen bow at 90 degrees when you enter.

---

### "Prove It: For Mankind" / 인류를 위하여 증명하시오
**Year / 연도:** 2014
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/prove-it-for-mankind/

Air bending versus the Large Hadron Collider. Why do few care about air bending while nations spend billions on the LHC at CERN?
장풍과 대형강입자충돌기. 수십조의 돈을 CERN의 LHC에 쓰면서 왜 아무도 장풍에 관심을 기울이지 않는가? 유사과학과 진짜 과학의 차이인가? 무엇이 세상을 더 좋게 만드는가에 대한 차이인가?

A curatorial research project and exhibition plan for the Asian Culture Center opening in Gwangju, Korea. Air bending versus the Large Hadron Collider. Why do few care about air bending while nations spend billions on the LHC at CERN? Is this about pseudoscience versus real science? Or about what actually improves the world? What is "betterment"? Experts prove; people believe—because most can't understand either way. The project places different "proofs" side by side: air bending vs. LHC, a man hiking with an atomic clock to feel time, human echolocation by blind people, a Dynasphere machine generating love Qi for world peace, quantum consciousness, and more. *The exhibition was never realized due to a change in director.

---

### ANTI-Climax / 용두사미 ANTI-Climax
**Year / 연도:** 2013
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/anti-climax/

A performance at the opening of MMCA Seoul. The show explored deconstructing the authority of previous artworks to create new ones.
용두사미 ANTI-Climax는 서울 국립현대미술관의 개관과 함께 선보인 공연이다. 기존에 얻어진 자신의 권위를 해체할 때 새로운 창작의 기회가 마련된다라는 주제로 기획되었다.

A performance at the opening of MMCA Seoul. Collaborating with actor Taeyoung Kang, actress Eun-ah Suh, and the band Idiotape, the show explored deconstructing the authority of previous artworks to create new ones. It began with LED poets spouting nonsense, moved through improvised confessions via computer chat, calling out every audience member's name, and flower-rope suicides, ending with loud music in the quiet museum. Deliberately clumsy graphics and fake sculptures were displayed prominently while technology stayed hidden. Only 3 of hundreds danced.

---

### Apple / 사과
**Year / 연도:** 2010–2018
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/apple/

The apple ripens when it receives attention. When someone photographs it with a flash, it slowly turns from green to red and emits eerie sounds.
사람들의 관심을 받을수록 익어가는 사과. 관객들이 플래시를 이용해 사진을 찍으면 사과는 이상한 소리를 내며 점점 빨갛게 익어간다. 빛에 반응하는 400개의 사운드 프로세서로 만들어져 독립적이면서도 싱크가 맞는 상태를 보여준다.

The apple ripens when it receives attention. When someone photographs it with a flash, it slowly turns from green to red and emits eerie sounds. 400 independent light and sound modules gradually synchronize.

---

### Uranium Necklace / 우라늄 목걸이
**Year / 연도:** 2010–2020
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/uranium-necklace/

Uranium Necklace is for those contemplating suicide—a way to taste death before making a final decision.
우라늄 목걸이는 자살하고 싶은 사람을 위한 작업이다. 최후의 결정을 내리기 전 죽음을 시음해 보자. 죽음을 시음하기 위해서는 좀 더 강한 물질이 필요하다. 마약 정도로는 충분하지 않다.

Uranium Necklace is for those contemplating suicide—a way to taste death before making a final decision. Drugs aren't potent enough; we need stronger materials. 

Upon purchase, the stainless steel wire necklace is locked so it cannot be removed. An attempt to sell one on eBay for $900,000 drew a comment that a life was worth less.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- The Penguin That Goes to the Mountain, NJP Art Center, Yongin, 2010
- Blackout Basel, Basel, Switzerland, 2015
  Uranium Necklace exhibited in an attic opened to the public for the first time in several hundred years.
  Some audiences contacted the organizers afterward to ask whether their health was safe.
  It was actually safe—the radiation level of the uranium ore was comparable to everyday exposure—but saying that would undermine the work.
- Death Tasting Live, Eulji Art Center, Seoul, 2020
  The Uranium Necklace is a necklace for people who wish to die, created by artist Hojun Song using actual uranium ore, gold, and stainless steel wire.
  As part of the “Eulji Drama” event, this program offers participants the opportunity to directly experience “death tasting” by wearing the Uranium Necklace for 10 minutes.
  Through one-on-one conversations with the artist about death, the event aims to explore clichéd yet fundamental questions in a lighthearted manner.
  A total of five participants will be selected, and each selected participant will be provided with a photograph of themselves wearing the Uranium Necklace.
  The event will be live-streamed online, and voice modulation and mosaic masking are available upon the participant’s request.

---

### The Strongest Weapon In The World / 이 세상에서 가장 강력한 무기
**Year / 연도:** 2007–2010
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/the-strongest-weapon-in-the-world/

A series of attempts to build an indestructible weapon that does NOT attack. When struck with a hammer, it says 'I love you.'
이 세상에서 가장 강력한 무기 프로젝트는 상대방을 공격하지 않으면서 절대 파괴되지 않는 무기들을 만드는 시도들을 보여준다. 2010년 선보인 'I Love You' 무기는 두꺼운 SC45 강철로 만들어졌고 공사장 망치로 내려치게 되면 내려친 사람에게 사랑한다 말해준다.

A series of attempts to build an indestructible weapon that does NOT attack. The first version, Weapons of Mass Happiness, was a heavy cast-aluminum sphere with a Geiger counter and a printer that outputs messages when triggered by nuclear explosion. 

The second was built with K-SAD students, featuring letters engraved on cow bones—emphasizing that indestructible data is the ultimate weapon. 

In 2010, the third version appeared: I Love You. Armored in thick SC45 steel with a piezo sensor inside, it says "I love you" when struck with a hammer.

**Presentations / 전시 이력:**
- Integrating, Asian Culture Center, Gwangju, 2007
  A series of attempts to build an indestructible weapon that does NOT attack. The first version, Weapons of Mass Happiness, was a heavy cast-aluminum sphere with a Geiger counter and a printer that outputs messages when triggered by nuclear explosion.
- Workshop 27, K-SAD,Gyeonggi, 2009
  Made with K-SAD students as the result of Workshop 27, Rosetta Stone 2009 features letters engraved on cow bones, suggesting that indestructible data is the ultimate weapon.
- The Lack of Electricity II, Space Can, Seoul, 2010
  In 2010, the third version of Strongest Weapon In the World appeared: I Love You. Armored in thick SC45 steel with a piezo sensor inside, it says "I love you" when struck with a hammer.

---

### Sync-O-Light / Sync-O-Light
**Year / 연도:** 2006–2017
**URL:** https://hojunsong.com/works/sync-o-light/

Inspired by Steven Strogatz's book SYNC, this project creates artificial fireflies using 200 sound-reactive UV light modules that shift between synchronized and chaotic states.
스티븐 스트로가츠의 책 『싱크』(2003)에 영감을 받아 만들어진 Sync-O-Light는 싱크와 카오스 상태를 반복하는 반딧불이들의 군집 반응을 표현하기 위해 서로 연결되지 않은 200여 개의 소형 프로세싱 시스템을 만들었다. 각 모듈은 소리에 반응하고 자외선 빛으로 표현한다.

Inspired by Steven Strogatz's book SYNC, this project creates artificial fireflies using 200 sound-reactive UV light modules that shift between synchronized and chaotic states.

---

- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@hojunsong
